question:
The number of accidents per month in a factory has been found to have a
geometric distribution with parameter p (probability of an accident at each
month), where the parameter is estimated by statistical methods. Let random
variable X is defined as the number of accidents in a given month. What is the
probability that there will be more than two accidents on a month? Calculate
when p = 0.1.
answer: Let X be a geometric random variable that represent the number of accidents in a given month with parameter p. $P(X>2)=\mathrm{p}^3=(0.1)^3=0.001$.
